items.title: Feature Tailr as project sponsor (README + terminal site) + add a Support section
items.description:
## Summary

The project has a real sponsor — **[Tailr](https://www.tailr.uk)** — but it isn't credited anywhere. The README has no sponsor/support section, and the site only exposes the Buy Me a Coffee (☕ SPONSOR) button, which is the *inverse* relationship (visitors funding the maintainer).

This adds an honest sponsor credit for Tailr and formalizes the existing support channels.

## Scope

**Website (`docs/terminal/app.jsx`)**
- New `SponsoredBy` badge — `◆ SPONSORED BY [T] tailr` with an inline SVG of Tailr's white-T mark, in the top-bar meta cluster **immediately left of** the existing ☕ SPONSOR button (unchanged).
- Links to https://www.tailr.uk (new tab, `rel=noopener`).
- Bump the `?v=` cache-bust token in `docs/index.html` so Pages serves the update.

**README.md** (hand-edited, outside the auto-synced `<!-- COUNT:* -->` markers — CI-safe)
- `## 💛 Sponsored by Tailr` block near the top (wordmark + honest one-liner + link).
- `## 💖 Support This Project` section (GitHub Sponsors + Buy Me a Coffee), mirroring `.github/FUNDING.yml`.

**Assets**
- `docs/assets/tailr-mark.svg`, `docs/assets/tailr-wordmark.png`.

## Honesty constraints
- Tailr is the maintainer's sibling product; its positioning explicitly calls for cross-promotion via the New-Grad-Jobs projects, so the credit is intended and truthful.
- Generic tech-company logos are **not** used as "sponsors". A separate, honest "Companies Hiring" logo wall is deferred to a follow-up.

## Non-goals
- Companies-hiring logo wall (follow-up PR).
- No change to the ☕ SPONSOR button meaning, footer, scraper, or `jobs.json`.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Tailr badge visible left of SPONSOR on the site, links to tailr.uk
- [ ] README has Sponsored-by-Tailr + Support sections
- [ ] Full CI green (pytest, pre-commit, codeql, pages build)

items.title: Add verified companies from data/analyst candidate batch (Greenhouse-probed)
items.description:
## Summary

A candidate batch of 23 data / research / analyst job rows was submitted for evaluation. Per the "only add if working" rule (see #316), each distinct company was probed against the ATS platforms the pipeline supports — Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, and Workday — and only companies with a **live, verified endpoint returning active jobs** should be added to `config.yml`.

## Candidate companies (23 rows → 22 distinct)

Civic Nation, CharterUP, Tata Consultancy Services, Third Bridge Group, Adelphi Research, MRM (×2), Instawork, Life Chiropractic College West, Ensemble Health Partners, UST, Precision Medicine Group, Langan Engineering & Environmental Services, C2Q Health Solutions, CoStar Group, New York University, Liberty University, Senior Helpers, Workday, Amrit Ocean Resort & Residences, DaVita Kidney Care, XiFin Inc., YAMMII LLC.

## Probe results

### ✅ Verified working — to be added (Greenhouse, live jobs)

| Company | Slug | Jobs |
|---------|------|-----:|
| CharterUP | `charterup` | 13 |
| Civic Nation | `civicnation` | 7 |
| Instawork | `instawork` | 54 |
| Precision Medicine Group | `precisionmedicinegroup` | 167 |
| Third Bridge (Group) | `thirdbridge` | 115 |

### Already present (skipped)

- **Workday** — the software company is already tracked under `apis.workday.companies`. (Row 19 "Sr Cloud Engineer / Workday" is a role *at* Workday.)

### Probed but rejected

- **Adelphi Research** — valid Greenhouse board (`adelphiresearch`) but **0 active postings**; nothing to surface, so left out until it has live jobs.
- **False-positive slugs** (belong to a *different* company): `costar` → Co–Star (astrology app, not CoStar Group real-estate), `ensemble` → a travel/tourism firm (not Ensemble Health Partners), `life` on Lever → LifeChurch, `new` → Sonja Inc., `tcs` → Thornbury Community Services (not Tata Consultancy Services).
- **No endpoint on a supported ATS**: Tata Consultancy Services, MRM, Life Chiropractic College West, Ensemble Health Partners, UST, Langan Engineering, C2Q Health Solutions, CoStar Group, New York University, Liberty University, Senior Helpers, Amrit Ocean Resort & Residences, DaVita Kidney Care, XiFin Inc., YAMMII LLC. (Most use iCIMS/Taleo/Workday tenants not currently wired, or are JS-rendered career portals.)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add the 5 verified Greenhouse companies to `config.yml` (alphabetically placed)
- [ ] `scripts/validate_config.py` passes
- [ ] Duplicate-URL guard passes
- [ ] `pytest tests/` passes
- [ ] CI green on the PR

Relates to #316.

items.title: Add hiring companies from 2268–2290 batch: Scout AI, SIXGEN, Booz Allen Hamilton
items.description:
## Summary

Second batch review of a 23-row job list (positions 2268–2290). Deduplicated to 20 unique companies, filtered out entries already tracked and those without a supported ATS endpoint. Added the 3 companies with confirmed live endpoints.

## Verified & adding

| Company | ATS | Endpoint | Live jobs* |
|---------|-----|----------|-----------:|
| Scout AI | Greenhouse | `boards-api.greenhouse.io/v1/boards/scoutai/jobs` | 23 |
| SIXGEN | Greenhouse | `boards-api.greenhouse.io/v1/boards/sixgeninc/jobs` | 23 |
| Booz Allen Hamilton | Workday | `bah.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/BAH_Jobs` | 1957 |

*Probed 2026-07-02.

## Already configured (no-op)

- CACI International — already in Workday section
- Leidos (incl. Leidos Australia) — already in Workday section
- Amazon (incl. AWS) — already in Workday section
- x.ai / xAI — already in Greenhouse section

## Intentionally not added

Each of the following was probed against the ATSs the pipeline currently supports (Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Workday). None returned a working slug with real job data:

- **ISI** — no ATS slug resolves; ambiguous name (multiple companies).
- **Capital Insurance Group** — no matching Greenhouse/Ashby/Lever board; `ciginsurance.com/careers` returns a placeholder.
- **City of Plano** — municipal government portal, no supported ATS.
- **Akima** — uses iCIMS (`careers-akima.icims.com`), not wired into the fetcher.
- **Mayo Clinic Healthcare London** — hospital research fellowship, not a corporate ATS.
- **SchoolSpring** — a job-board marketplace, not an employer with a company board.
- **Authentic (Authentic Brands Group)** — no matching Greenhouse/Ashby/Lever slug; careers page has no discoverable ATS API.
- **Mutual of Omaha** — careers site is a JS-rendered SPA proxied via `careers.mutualofomaha.com`; no supported ATS endpoint.
- **Notability / Ginger Labs** — no ATS API found; careers page returns 404.
- **Quest Global** — no matching Workday tenant resolves; iCIMS/other providers not wired in.
- **Cecil College / New York University** — university, not a corporate ATS.
- **The Vermont Country Store** — no supported ATS endpoint discovered.

Per the "only add if working" rule, these are omitted until a supported endpoint is identified.

## Verification

- `python scripts/validate_config.py` — TOTAL: 281 companies, all sections load
- Duplicate-URL guard — 281 unique URLs, no duplicates
- `pytest tests/` — 733 passed

## Test plan

- [x] Config validates
- [x] No duplicate API URLs
- [x] Unit tests pass locally
- [ ] CI green on PR

items.title: Consider Hanzilla Jobs as a Canada-specific new-grad companion resource
items.description:
Hi — thanks for maintaining this automated new-grad list.

I’m building Hanzilla Jobs, a free daily-updated Canadian student/recent-grad jobs board: https://jobs.hanzilla.co/new-grad/

It is not meant to replace this repo, but it may be a useful Canada-specific companion resource for students who land here while looking for early-career roles. Hanzilla focuses on Canadian internships, co-ops, new grad, junior, and entry-level roles across software, data/ML, engineering, business, finance, sciences, and other student-relevant fields.

A couple relevant deep links:

- New grad / junior roles: https://jobs.hanzilla.co/new-grad/
- Canada internships / co-ops: https://jobs.hanzilla.co/internships/
- About / verification page: https://jobs.hanzilla.co/about/

If you think it fits, would you consider adding a small “Canada-specific companion resources” or “Related resources” note somewhere in the README/docs? No worries if that is outside the scope of this repo.

Disclosure: I’m the builder of Hanzilla Jobs.

items.title: [ROADMAP] Help us expand job data — all suggestions welcome!
items.description:
## TL;DR

We want to dramatically expand the job data this project surfaces, and we need **your ideas**. Whether you have a small tweak, a wild idea, or a full proposal — drop it in the comments. **All suggestions are welcome and I will reply to every comment.**

---

## Why this roadmap?

New-Grad-Jobs is most useful when it captures the widest possible surface of legitimate new-grad opportunities. Today we scrape and curate from a known set of sources, but there's a long tail of companies, regions, and job types we're missing.

This issue is the central place to brainstorm **how we add more jobs** — sources, regions, scrapers, partnerships, automation, data quality, anything.

## What kind of suggestions are we looking for?

Pick whatever resonates — these are just prompts:

### Sources
- [ ] Specific ATS platforms we don't yet scrape (Workday tenants, Greenhouse boards, Lever, Ashby, SmartRecruiters, iCIMS, Taleo, Eightfold, Phenom, Jobvite, BrassRing, etc.)
- [ ] Specific companies whose new-grad pipelines we should track
- [ ] Regional job boards (EU, India, LATAM, MENA, APAC, Canada, UK, etc.)
- [ ] University career portals worth aggregating
- [ ] Government / public-sector hiring portals
- [ ] Startup-focused aggregators (YC, Wellfound, etc.)
- [ ] Diversity-focused or accessibility-focused boards

### Job categories
- [ ] Internship pipelines distinct from full-time
- [ ] PhD / research / quant-specific listings
- [ ] Hardware, semiconductor, robotics, aerospace
- [ ] Game industry
- [ ] Finance / trading / actuarial new-grad tracks
- [ ] Bio / pharma / healthcare new-grad roles
- [ ] Trades, ops, supply chain, manufacturing new-grad programs

### Data quality
- [ ] How should we detect & dedupe reposted listings?
- [ ] How should we classify \"new grad\" vs \"junior\" vs \"intern\" more accurately?
- [ ] Should we surface salary data when available? From which sources?
- [ ] Should we flag visa sponsorship / remote / hybrid more explicitly?
- [ ] How should we handle stale postings (last-seen vs first-seen)?
- [ ] What metadata is most useful to filter on?

### Infrastructure & automation
- [ ] Scraper frameworks or libraries worth adopting
- [ ] Anti-bot / rate-limit strategies that respect ToS
- [ ] Workflow scheduling improvements (cron, GitHub Actions, queues)
- [ ] LLM-assisted parsing for unstructured careers pages
- [ ] Storage / dedup / canonicalization approaches
- [ ] Notification channels (RSS, Discord, Slack, email digest)

### Community & contribution
- [ ] How can we make it easier for contributors to add a new company in one PR?
- [ ] Should we publish a contribution template specifically for \"add a company\"?
- [ ] Would a structured YAML/JSON registry of companies + ATS endpoints help?
- [ ] Mentorship for first-time contributors who want to add a scraper

## How to suggest

Just comment below. Any format works:

- **A single company:** \"Please add `<Company>` — their careers page is `<URL>`\"
- **A source idea:** \"There's an ATS called `<X>` used by these companies: …\"
- **A feature idea:** \"It would be great if listings showed `<field>`\"
- **A full proposal:** longer-form design ideas, link to a gist or doc
- **A pain point:** \"As a job seeker, I wish this repo did `<X>`\"

You don't need to know how to scrape, write Python, or anything technical. **Ideas count just as much as code.**

## Commitment

- I'll **reply to every comment on this thread.**
- Concrete, actionable suggestions will be moved into their own tracking issues and linked back here.
- Larger themes will become milestones on the roadmap.
- Credit will go to the suggester in the resulting PR / issue / changelog.

## Ground rules

- Be respectful — this is a community space.
- Don't post anything that violates a source's Terms of Service or robots.txt; we want sustainable, ethical aggregation.
- Don't post personal data, recruiter contacts, or anything that could harm a company or candidate.
- Prefer one idea per comment when possible — it makes follow-up easier.

---

**Thanks in advance — every suggestion helps a new grad find their first job.**

items.title: [Feature]: add publish-time URL safety validation for jobs artifact output
items.description:
### Problem
The pipeline validates schema/contracts but does not enforce a strict publish-time URL safety gate before writing `docs/jobs.json`. Unsafe or malformed links (non-http schemes, localhost/private-network targets, invalid hosts) could be published into public artifacts.

### Proposed Solution
Add publish-time URL safety validation before artifact write/finalization:
- allow only `http`/`https`
- reject localhost/private IP ranges/metadata endpoints/malformed hosts
- surface blocked-count telemetry in health output
- optionally fail fast or mark degraded when violations exceed a threshold

### Alternatives Considered
- Rely on liveness checks only (too late: unsafe links may already be published).
- Manual cleanup through issue reports (reactive and inconsistent).

### 🏗️ Which area does this affect?
🤖 Core scraper (scripts/update_jobs.py)

### Constraints Check
- [x] This does NOT require an external database (PostgreSQL, Redis, SQLite, etc.)
- [x] This does NOT require a frontend framework (React, Vue, Next.js, Tailwind)
- [x] This does NOT require an external orchestrator (Airflow, Celery, etc.)
items.excerpt: ### Problem The pipeline validates schema/contracts but does not enforce a strict publish-time URL safety gate before writing `docs/jobs.json`. Unsafe or malformed links (non-http schemes, localhost/private-network ta...

items.title: [Feature]: add automated duplicate-issue guardrail for triage quality
items.description:
### Problem
Issue backlog quality degrades when near-duplicate issues are opened (for example, repeated or reintroduced issue topics). This increases maintainer triage overhead and fragments context.

### Proposed Solution
Add a lightweight duplicate-detection guardrail in issue triage:
- on issue open/edit, compare against recent open + closed issue titles/bodies
- if similarity is high, auto-comment candidate duplicates and add a `possible-duplicate` label
- keep final decision manual (no auto-close)

This should preserve discussion quality while remaining maintainer-friendly.

### Alternatives Considered
Manual duplicate checks only.
This works at small scale but does not scale with automation-generated and community issue volume.

### 🏗️ Which area does this affect?
🔁 GitHub Actions / CI-CD (.github/workflows/)

### Constraints Check
- [x] This does NOT require an external database (PostgreSQL, Redis, SQLite, etc.)
- [x] This does NOT require a frontend framework (React, Vue, Next.js, Tailwind)
- [x] This does NOT require an external orchestrator (Airflow, Celery, etc.)

items.title: [Good First Issue]: align ci.yml config validation with current config["apis"] schema
items.description:
---
## Welcome to New Grad Jobs! 🎓

This template creates a **Good First Issue** — a small, guided task that helps new
contributors learn the codebase and contribution workflow.

**Good First Issues are pre-approved by the maintainer.** A contributor can claim it
immediately by commenting `/assign`.

---

### 🆕 Newcomer Friendly

This is a **Good First Issue** — a guided, well-scoped task designed for contributors
who are new to this repository.

#### What you'll do
- ✅ Read a small part of the codebase
- ✅ Make a focused, well-defined change
- ✅ Write or extend a test
- ✅ Submit and merge a pull request

#### Support
The maintainer actively monitors Good First Issues and will respond to questions
within 24–48 hours.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **This issue does not require prior scraping or API experience.**
>
> - Basic Python and Git are sufficient
> - You must be assigned before starting — comment `/assign` to claim this issue
> - Read [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/ambicuity/New-Grad-Jobs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
>   before opening a PR

> [!NOTE]
> ⏱️ **Typical time to complete:** 30–90 minutes (once local setup is done)
> 🧩 **Difficulty:** Small, well-contained change
> 🎓 **Best for:** First-time contributors

**🏁 When this issue is complete, you will have:**
- ✅ A merged PR in a real open-source project
- ✅ Your name in the [Contributors Hall of Fame](https://github.com/ambicuity/New-Grad-Jobs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTORS.md)
- ✅ Confidence to take on larger issues next

> [!IMPORTANT]
> #### 📋 What makes a Good First Issue in this repo?
>
> **Often a good fit:**
> - Adding a missing company to `config.yml` (Greenhouse, Lever endpoint)
> - Fixing a keyword in `categorize_job()` or `get_company_tier()`
> - Correcting a typo or broken link in documentation
> - Adding a test case for an existing, already-working function
> - Small CSS/HTML improvements to `docs/`
>
> **Not a good fit for GFI:**
> - Changes to `filter_jobs()`, `deduplicate_jobs()`, or date parsing
> - New scraping source integrations (Workday, Google Careers)
> - Architectural changes to `update_jobs.py`
>
> Those belong in **Beginner**, **Intermediate**, or **Advanced** issues.

### 👾 Task Description

The `Validate config.yml structure` step in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` reads company counts from outdated key paths:

- Current code reads `config.get("greenhouse", {})` and `config.get("lever", {})`
- Actual schema stores these under `config["apis"]["greenhouse"]` and `config["apis"]["lever"]`

Because of this mismatch, the workflow prints incorrect counts and does not validate the nested schema as intended.

Please update the inline validation script to match the current config structure.

Suggested scope:
- Ensure required top-level keys include `filtering`, `apis`, and `readme`
- Read company lists from `config["apis"]["greenhouse"]["companies"]` and `config["apis"]["lever"]["companies"]`
- Keep behavior simple and non-breaking: fail only on true schema problems
- Keep output logs clear for contributors debugging CI

**Area touched:**
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

### ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CI validation step reads Greenhouse/Lever company counts from `config["apis"]`
- [ ] Required key checks include `apis`
- [ ] Validation output reflects real company counts from current schema
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (`make test`)
- [ ] PR is linked to this issue with `Fixes #<number>`
- [ ] PR title follows Conventional Commits format

items.title: [Beginner]: Add an `.editorconfig` file to standardize code formatting
items.description:
### Description
Create an `.editorconfig` at the root of the repository to enforce 4 spaces for Python, 2 spaces for JSON/YAML, UTF-8 charset, and final newlines.

### Why it matters
Ensures that all contributors automatically use the correct tab/space settings in their IDEs before they even hit pre-commit hooks.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create `.editorconfig`
- [ ] Define rules for `*.py`, `*.yml`, `*.md`
- [ ] Ensure it enforces trim-trailing-whitespace and final newlines
- [ ] Passes `pre-commit run --all-files`

items.title: [Beginner]: Make `normalize_date_string` accept an optional `reference_date` parameter
items.description:
### Description
Currently, `normalize_date_string` uses `datetime.now()` directly. This makes it hard to test deterministically. Add an optional `reference_date` parameter that defaults to `None`.

### Why it matters
Injecting the current time via a parameter allows tests to pass fixed dates, eliminating flaky tests around midnight boundaries.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add `reference_date: datetime = None` parameter
- [ ] Use `reference_date or datetime.now()` inside the function
- [ ] Update tests if necessary
- [ ] Passes `pre-commit run --all-files`

items.title: [Beginner]: Add explicit docstrings and type hints to fix_nan_only.py
items.description: The fix_json_file function in fix_nan_only.py lacks a formal docstring explaining its purpose, arguments, and return value. Additionally, adding type hints for the filepath argument will improve maintainability.
items.excerpt: The fix_json_file function in fix_nan_only.py lacks a formal docstring explaining its purpose, arguments, and return value. Additionally, adding type hints for the filepath argument will improve maintainability.

items.title: [Beginner]: Add argparse to generate_companies.py for command-line options
items.description: Currently, generate_companies.py hardcodes the number of companies to generate (e.g., 4000, 1900, 1300) in the __main__ block. This issue is to use argparse to allow these to be passed as command-line arguments (e.g., --greenhouse-count 4000).
